{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_0","type":"agent_message","text":"Break the import from `src/domain/billing.ts` to `src/presentation/invoice-view.ts`.

Why: that edge is both part of the 3-file cycle and the separate architecture violation:

```text
src/application/billing-flow.ts
  -> src/domain/billing.ts
  -> src/presentation/invoice-view.ts
  -> src/application/billing-flow.ts
```

The domain layer should not depend on presentation. Keeping `billing.ts` pure prevents UI code from being pulled into domain initialization, which is the most likely source of the circular runtime failure.

Best fix: move whatever `billing.ts` needs from `invoice-view.ts` into a neutral module or domain-owned abstraction. For example:

- shared display types/helpers: move to `src/domain/billing-format.ts` or `src/shared/invoice-types.ts`
- UI rendering behavior: invert the dependency so `invoice-view.ts` imports from `billing.ts`, not the other way around
- type-only need: change to `import type` if no runtime value is needed

I would not break the `billing-flow.ts -> billing.ts` edge first, because application depending on domain is normal. The suspicious edge is `domain -> presentation`; removing it fixes the cycle and restores the intended layer direction."}}
